Meta.title.context: Browser-tab / PWA title. “Numbers” stays verbatim. “CFCC” is the ENGLISH abbreviation only — Chinese uses the church's Chinese name, 中华归主海沃教会 / 中華歸主海沃教會 (see GLOSSARY).

Meta.description.source: Snap church receipts now, submit reimbursement claims later.
Meta.description.context: Meta description shown in link previews and the PWA install sheet.

Meta.appName.context: iOS home-screen name. Always “Numbers” — never translated.

Common.status.draft.context: Status pill: claim not yet generated, still editable. Pairs with 'Generated'. One word.

Common.status.generated.context: Status pill: PDF has been generated, rows frozen. Pairs with 'Draft'. Keep short.

Common.status.needsReview.context: Status pill on the claims list for drafts awaiting verification.

Common.status.processed.context: Lowercase on purpose — appended mid-sentence to the receipt card's date · size line. Review.revertConfirm quotes this wording (test-enforced).

Common.status.submitted.context: Claim status chip: sent to an approver, waiting for their signature.

Common.status.approved.context: Claim status chip: the approver signed off; payment still pending.

Common.status.rejected.context: Claim status chip: the approver declined and sent it back.

Common.status.paid.context: Claim status chip: the treasurer recorded payment. Terminal state.

Common.cancel.context: Secondary dialog button: close without saving. One word.

Common.back.context: Generic Back button in multi-step dialogs (returns to the previous step).

Common.save.context: Primary button that commits a form or dialog. One word.

Common.saving.context: Save button label while the request is in flight. Keep the trailing ellipsis.

Common.loading.context: Placeholder text shown while a page's data loads.

Common.language.context: Label for the language selectors (NavBar switcher aria + profile field).

Common.role.member.context: Church role shown in pickers/chips: ordinary member with no special powers.

Common.role.approver.context: Church role: a member authorized to approve reimbursement claims.

Common.role.treasurer.context: Church role: handles payments (writes the checks).

Common.role.admin.context: Church role: the administrator who anchors electronic signing.

NavBar.shoebox.context: Top-navigation link. Auto-copied from Shoebox.title — translate that key.

NavBar.claims.context: Top-navigation link. Auto-copied from Claims.title — translate that key.

NavBar.profile.context: Top-navigation link. Auto-copied from Profile.title — translate that key.

NavBar.signOut.context: Nav button that ends the session. Keep short.

NavBar.signedInAs.context: Tooltip on the sign-out button; {name} is the user's display name.

NavBar.approvals.context: Top-nav link to the approver's inbox. Keep short — phone-width nav.

NavBar.finance.context: Top-nav link to the treasurer's payment queue. Keep short — phone-width nav.

Home.profileNudge.source: Your <link>profile</link> needs a name and mailing address — both are printed on the reimbursement form.
Home.profileNudge.context: Warning banner on the home page; <link> wraps the word that opens the profile page.

SignIn.tagline.source: CFCC expense reimbursements — snap receipts now, file the claim later.
SignIn.tagline.context: One-liner under the app name on the sign-in card. English uses the CFCC abbreviation; Chinese uses the church's full Chinese name.

SignIn.failed.context: Generic sign-in failure; also the fallback when the server gives no better message.

SignIn.popupBlocked.source: Your browser blocked the sign-in popup. If you opened this from inside another app, tap the menu and choose “Open in Safari” (or Chrome), then try again.
SignIn.popupBlocked.context: Error when the browser blocks the Google sign-in popup; instructs escaping an in-app browser.

SignIn.embeddedTitle.context: Heading of the warning box shown inside in-app browsers (Messenger, Instagram…).

SignIn.embeddedBody.source: Google sign-in may not work in in-app browsers. If it doesn't, open this page in Safari or Chrome — tap the menu (⋯ or the share icon) and choose “Open in Safari”.
SignIn.embeddedBody.context: Body of that warning; ⋯ is the browser's menu glyph — keep it.

SignIn.googleSignIn.context: Primary sign-in button (Google logo precedes the text).

SignIn.googleTryAnyway.context: Same button while inside an in-app browser, where sign-in will likely fail.

SignIn.notConfigured.source: No sign-in method is configured. Set FIREBASE_API_KEY, FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN and FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID in the environment.
SignIn.notConfigured.context: Operator-facing notice; <code> wraps environment-variable names — never translate them.

SignIn.devLogin.context: Heading of the passwordless login form that only exists in test mode.

SignIn.devEmailPlaceholder.context: Example email placeholder; usually kept as-is.

SignIn.devNamePlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the dev-login name input.

SignIn.devSignIn.context: Submit button of the dev-login form; '(dev)' marks it as test-only.

Claims.title.context: Page title of the claims list. Canonical — the NavBar link auto-copies this value.

Claims.subtitle.source: Draft claims need review; generated claims are ready to print and sign.
Claims.subtitle.context: One-liner under the title explaining draft vs generated.

Claims.emptyTitle.context: Empty-state heading when the user has no claims yet.

Claims.emptyBody.source: Select receipts on the <link>Receipts</link> page and hit “New Claim”.
Claims.emptyBody.context: Empty-state body; <link> opens the Shoebox. Quotes the New Claim button — its exact current translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Claims.counts.source: {items} items · {receipts, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}
Claims.counts.context: Meta line per claim: {items} row count, {receipts} receipt count (ICU).

Profile.title.context: Page title. Canonical — the NavBar link auto-copies this value.

Profile.subtitle.source: Printed onto the “Name” and “Address” lines of the reimbursement form.
Profile.subtitle.context: Explains the fields are printed onto the official (English) PDF form; the quoted words are that form's lines.

Profile.fullName.context: Field label for the name printed on the reimbursement check.

Profile.fullNamePlaceholder.context: Example person name — use a natural name for the locale.

Profile.mailingAddress.context: Field label for the check-mailing address.

Profile.mailingAddressPlaceholder.context: Example US postal address; typically kept as-is.

Profile.saved.context: Inline confirmation next to Save; keep the ✓.

Profile.loadFailed.context: Error when the profile fetch fails.

Profile.saveFailed.context: Fallback error when saving fails without a server message.

ReceiptGrid.pdfChip.context: Tiny chip on a card when a PDF thumbnail can't render; 'PDF' acronym stays.

ReceiptGrid.pdfThumbAlt.context: Alt text (screen readers) of a PDF receipt's thumbnail image.

ReceiptGrid.deleteReceipt.context: aria-label of the ✕ delete button on a receipt card; {name} is the uploaded file name.

ReceiptGrid.viewLarger.context: aria-label of the expand button on a card; {name} is the file name.

ReceiptGrid.viewLargerTitle.context: Hover tooltip of that expand button. Keep short.

ReceiptGrid.notePlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the free-text description input on every receipt card.

ReceiptGrid.noteAria.context: aria-label of that description input; {name} is the file name.

ReceiptGrid.meta.context: Card meta line: upload date · file size. Keep the ' · ' separators and 'KB'.

ReceiptGrid.claimChip.context: Chip on a card linking to a GENERATED claim it belongs to; drafts use Common.status.draft instead. One word.

PdfPreview.chip.context: Fallback chip when an inline PDF preview fails; 'PDF' stays.

PdfPreview.rendering.context: Auto-copied from Viewer.rendering — translate that key.

PdfPreview.pageAlt.context: Alt text of each preview page image; {page} is the page number.

PdfPreview.omitted.source: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — open the PDF receipt to view it.} other {# more pages not shown — open the PDF receipt to view them.}}
PdfPreview.omitted.context: Truncation note under a long PDF's preview (ICU).

PdfPreview.open.context: Link under the preview to the original PDF; keep the ↗.

Shoebox.title.context: Title of the receipt-capture page ('Receipts'). Internally still called Shoebox (component, keys, testids). Canonical — the NavBar link auto-copies this value. NOTE: also the plain word for receipts, so location phrases quote it (“收据”页 / 「收據」頁).

Shoebox.intro.source: Drop receipts here as you go. Select some when you're ready to file a claim.

Shoebox.upload.context: Primary upload button; keep the 📷. Canonical — the add-receipts dialog auto-copies it.

Shoebox.uploading.context: Upload button while a file is in flight; keep the ellipsis. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.uploadFailed.context: Error when an upload fails. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.dropTitle.context: Heading of the full-screen drag-and-drop overlay.

Shoebox.dropBody.context: Subline of the drop overlay: accepted file kinds.

Shoebox.noteSaveFailed.context: Error when saving a card's description fails.

Shoebox.deleteConfirm.context: Native browser confirm() text before deleting a receipt.

Shoebox.deleteFailed.context: Error when deleting a receipt fails.

Shoebox.selectedCount.source: {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} selected
Shoebox.selectedCount.context: Sticky-bar label while receipts are selected (ICU count).

Shoebox.selectPrompt.source: Select receipts below to start a claim
Shoebox.selectPrompt.context: Sticky-bar label when nothing is selected yet.

Shoebox.newClaim.context: THE primary call-to-action; keep the ✨. Claims.emptyBody and Shoebox.step2 quote this wording (minus ✨) — changing it re-drafts them (test-enforced).

Shoebox.waitingRateLimit.context: New Claim button label while the AI provider is rate-limited.

Shoebox.readingShort.context: New Claim button label while extraction runs.

Shoebox.selectHint.context: Appears under the button after clicking with nothing selected; ↑ points at the receipt cards above — keep it.

Shoebox.readingInitial.context: Live status line when extraction starts (count not yet known). Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.readingCount.source: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
Shoebox.readingCount.context: Live status line once the receipt count is known (ICU). Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.readProgress.context: Live progress line: {completed} of {total} receipts done. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.manualInstead.context: Escape-hatch button during a rate-limit wait: skip the AI, type rows by hand. Canonical for the add-receipts dialog.

Shoebox.manualSetup.context: Status line while the manual (no-AI) claim is being created.

Shoebox.manualStartFailed.context: Error when that manual claim can't be created.

Shoebox.generateEnded.context: Error when the progress stream ends without a result.

Shoebox.emptyTitle.context: Empty-state heading for a brand-new user (no receipts uploaded yet).

Shoebox.emptyBody.source: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to get started.
Shoebox.emptyBody.context: Empty-state subline inviting the first upload.

Shoebox.step1.source: <step>1. Snap.</step> Photograph receipts the moment you buy.
Shoebox.step1.context: Onboarding step 1 of 4; <step> wraps the numbered lead word — keep the 'N. Verb.' pattern.

Shoebox.step2.source: <step>2. Batch.</step> Later, when you're ready, select receipts and hit New Claim.
Shoebox.step2.context: Onboarding step 2; <step> wraps the numbered lead word. Quotes the New Claim button — its exact translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Shoebox.step3.source: <step>3. Verify.</step> Check every row against the receipt. Fix, split, or exclude items.
Shoebox.step3.context: Onboarding step 3: the human verification pass.

Shoebox.step4.source: <step>4. Print.</step> Download the filled CFCC form with receipts attached, sign, and drop it off.
Shoebox.step4.context: Onboarding step 4; 'the filled CFCC form' means the church's official reimbursement form — Chinese says 教会报销表 / 教會報銷表 rather than the English abbreviation.

Shoebox.processedSummary.context: Collapsible section heading for receipts already on generated claims; {count}.

Shoebox.processedNote.source: Already on a generated claim. You can still pick it if part of the purchase belongs on another claim.
Shoebox.processedNote.context: Fine print under that heading: processed receipts stay selectable.

Shoebox.prepareTitle.context: Heading of the per-photo dialog shown before anything uploads.

Shoebox.prepareLeft.context: Suffix after that heading when more files queue: (N left). Keep the parentheses.

Shoebox.noteLabel.context: Label of the optional description input in the prepare dialog.

Shoebox.notePlaceholder.context: Example description; 'VBS' (Vacation Bible School) stays verbatim.

Shoebox.prepareHintLocal.source: Uploads when you save. You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.prepareHintLocal.context: Fine print for photos: nothing uploads until Save.

Shoebox.prepareHintUploaded.source: You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.prepareHintUploaded.context: Fine print for PDFs (already uploaded when picked).

Shoebox.skipAll.context: Dialog button: upload ALL queued photos without descriptions. It skips the notes, not the uploads.

Shoebox.quotaRingAria.source: Retrying in about {seconds, plural, one {# second} other {# seconds}}
Shoebox.quotaRingAria.context: aria-label of the countdown ring during a rate-limit wait (ICU seconds).

Viewer.ariaTitle.context: aria-label of the full-screen receipt viewer dialog; {name} is the file name.

Viewer.openNewTab.context: Tooltip + aria of the ↗ icon button.

Viewer.closeTitle.context: Tooltip of ✕; 'Esc' is the keyboard key name — keep it.

Viewer.previewUnavailable.source: Preview unavailable — use ↗ to open the PDF
Viewer.previewUnavailable.context: Empty state when a PDF preview fails; ↗ refers to the open-in-new-tab icon — keep it.

Viewer.rendering.context: Spinner text while the server rasterizes a PDF. Canonical — the inline PDF preview auto-copies it.

Viewer.pageAlt.context: Alt text of each rendered PDF page; {name} file name, {page} number.

Viewer.omitted.source: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.} other {# more pages not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.}}
Viewer.omitted.context: Note when a long PDF is truncated (ICU); ↗ refers to the icon — keep it.

Viewer.editTitle.context: Tooltip of that button. Keep short.

Viewer.editButton.context: Auto-copied from Review.editPhotoButton — translate that key.

Viewer.zoomOut.context: Tooltip + aria of the − zoom button.

Viewer.zoomIn.context: Tooltip + aria of the + zoom button.

Viewer.resetZoom.context: Tooltip + aria of the reset-zoom button.

Viewer.zoomLevel.context: Zoom badge between the buttons; {percent} is a number — keep the %.

ManualEntry.title.context: Dialog heading when the AI could not read a receipt and the user types it in.

ManualEntry.intro.source: The AI couldn't read {name}. Type in what's printed on it so you can verify the row.
ManualEntry.intro.context: Dialog intro; <strong> wraps {name}, the receipt's file name.

ManualEntry.openPdf.context: Fallback link when the browser can't embed the PDF.

ManualEntry.merchant.context: Field label: store/vendor name as printed on the receipt. One word.

ManualEntry.merchantPlaceholder.context: Example merchant; a recognizable real store name works best.

ManualEntry.purchaseDate.context: Field label for the date printed on the receipt.

ManualEntry.optional.context: Grey suffix after a field label; keep the parentheses.

ManualEntry.printedTotal.context: Field label; ($) = amount entered in dollars — keep the $.

ManualEntry.refunded.context: Field label for the refunded amount; keep the ($).

ManualEntry.amountPlaceholder.context: Numeric placeholder for money inputs; usually kept as 0.00.

ManualEntry.summary.context: Field label for the short list of items bought.

ManualEntry.summaryPlaceholder.source: e.g. Paper towels, snack variety pack, folding table

ManualEntry.rowAmount.context: Computed net line under the fields; <strong> wraps {amount}, a pre-formatted $ value.

ManualEntry.totalMinusRefunded.context: Parenthetical appended to rowAmount only when a refund exists. Keep the LEADING SPACE and the − sign.

ManualEntry.later.context: Secondary button: close the dialog and fill the row in later.

ImageEditor.title.context: Dialog heading of the rotate/crop tool.

ImageEditor.introRestoring.source: Showing the originally uploaded image. Save to keep it, or Cancel to leave the current one.
ImageEditor.introRestoring.context: Intro while previewing the original image restore.

ImageEditor.introLocal.source: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background.
ImageEditor.introLocal.context: Intro in the pre-upload flow: the edit happens on-device.

ImageEditor.introStored.source: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background. Saving replaces the stored image.
ImageEditor.introStored.context: Intro when editing an already-uploaded photo.

ImageEditor.reset.context: Button clearing the pending edit / staging the original. One word.

ImageEditor.resetTitle.source: Reset to the originally uploaded image

ImageEditor.editedAlt.context: Alt text of the photo being edited.

ImageEditor.loadFailed.source: Could not load the receipt image
ImageEditor.loadFailed.context: Error when the image can't be loaded into the editor.

ImageEditor.editFailed.context: Fallback error when saving the edit fails.

ImageEditor.saveDisabledTitle.source: Rotate or draw a crop first
ImageEditor.saveDisabledTitle.context: Tooltip on the disabled Save button before any change was made.

Review.loadFailed.context: Error when the claim fetch fails.

Review.backToClaims.context: Link inside that error banner, back to the claims list.

Review.updateFailed.context: Generic fallback when a row/claim edit fails.

Review.mergeFailed.context: Fallback when merging split rows fails.

Review.title.context: Page heading; the Draft/Generated status pill renders right after it.

Review.instruction.source: Check each amount against what you actually paid, pick a ministry, then check it off.
Review.instruction.context: One-liner under the heading describing the verification job.

Review.receiptHeader.context: Per-receipt group header; {index} is 1-based, {label} is 'Merchant — date' or a file name.

Review.headerNote.context: Suffix after the header showing the user's note; keep the '· ' separator.

Review.removeDisabledTitle.source: This is the only receipt — discard the claim instead
Review.removeDisabledTitle.context: Tooltip of the disabled Remove button when it's the claim's only receipt.

Review.removeTitle.source: Remove receipt from claim (back to the Receipts page)
Review.removeTitle.context: Tooltip of the enabled Remove button; the receipt goes back to the Receipts page.

Review.removeButton.context: Button removing a receipt from the claim; keep the ✕. Keep short.

Review.derivation.context: Banner explaining the AI's net amount; all three {args} are pre-formatted $ values. Keep the − and → symbols.

Review.editPhotoTitle.source: Rotate or crop this receipt photo
Review.editPhotoTitle.context: Tooltip of the rotate/crop button on the claim review screen.

Review.editPhotoButton.context: Rotate/crop button label; keep the ✂. Canonical — the viewer button auto-copies it.

Review.receiptNote.context: Note line above a single-receipt claim's rows; <strong> wraps {note}, the user's own text.

Review.aiFailed.source: ⚠ The AI couldn't read this receipt.
Review.aiFailed.context: Warning banner when extraction failed for this receipt; keep the ⚠.

Review.enterDetails.context: Button in that banner opening the manual-entry dialog. Keep short.

Review.subtotal.context: Label before the per-receipt subtotal amount; keep the trailing colon.

Review.claimTotal.context: Label of the claim's grand-total row.

Review.verifiedProgress.context: Progress text next to the bar; keep the '{verified} / {total}' shape.

Review.generatedFrozen.context: Status line replacing the progress bar once the claim is generated.

Review.addReceipts.context: Action-bar button opening the add-receipts dialog; keep the full-width ＋.

Review.discard.context: Action-bar button that deletes the draft claim; keep it to one or two words.

Review.revert.context: Action-bar button on generated claims: back to editable draft. Keep the ↩.

Review.chooseMinistryFirst.context: Tooltip on the disabled verify button AND the gated PDF button.

Review.buildingPdf.context: PDF button label while busy — covers both generating a new PDF and downloading the already-signed packet, so keep it generic (not “generating”). Keep the ellipsis.

Review.printAction.context: Finish action on the review action bar: generates/downloads the packet to print & hand-sign. Sole primary when e-sign is off; secondary beside the E-sign primary when it's on. Terse, no emoji.

Review.esignAction.context: Primary finish action on the e-sign action bar: opens the electronic-signature ceremony. Terse (pairs with the Print secondary).

Review.addReceiptShort.context: Compact mobile label for the Add-receipts button in the action bar (desktop shows the fuller Review.addReceipts). Keep the leading + and singular noun.

Review.esignFinishHint.source: Finish this claim — print, or e-sign.
Review.esignFinishHint.context: One-line hint on the action bar for e-sign users on a single-receipt draft (desktop only), naming the two finish paths.

Review.revertConfirm.source: Revert this claim to draft? Only do this if you haven’t turned in the printed form yet. Rows become editable again, and the receipts leave “processed”.
Review.revertConfirm.context: Native confirm() before reverting. Quotes the 'processed' status word — its exact translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Review.removeConfirm.source: Remove this receipt from the claim? Its rows are deleted and the receipt goes back to the Receipts page.
Review.removeConfirm.context: Native confirm() before removing a receipt from the claim; mentions the Receipts page by name — match Shoebox.title's wording.

Review.discardConfirm.source: Discard this draft claim? Receipts go back to the Receipts page.
Review.discardConfirm.context: Native confirm() before deleting the draft claim; mentions the Receipts page by name — match Shoebox.title's wording.

Review.deleteFailed.context: Error when deleting the claim fails.

Review.pdfFailed.context: Fallback error when PDF generation fails.

Review.suggestionFailed.context: Fallback error when the AI suggestion call fails.

Review.fanOutSet.source: Set {label} on {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
Review.fanOutSet.context: Undo-toast after a claim-wide ministry change; {label} is Review.quotedValue or Review.noMinistry; ICU row count.

Review.quotedValue.context: Quote wrapper for a ministry name inside fanOutSet; use the script's quote marks (“” / 「」).

Review.noMinistry.context: Slots into fanOutSet's {label} when the ministry was cleared. Lowercase fragment, not a sentence.

Review.undo.context: Button on the fan-out toast and suggestion banner. One word.

Review.dismissAria.context: aria-label of the ✕ on the toast.

Review.splitModeTitle.context: Dialog heading when Split is pressed on a single-ministry claim.

Review.splitModeBody.source: This claim is set to use one ministry for every row.
Review.splitModeBody.context: Dialog body; <strong> emphasizes the one-ministry setting.

Review.splitModeBody2.source: Switch the claim to multiple ministries to split this row.
Review.splitModeBody2.context: Second paragraph: switching to multi-ministry enables the split.

Review.switchAndSplit.context: Primary button of that dialog: flip the mode, then open the split dialog.

Review.modeSwitchTitle.source: Use one ministry for the whole claim?
Review.modeSwitchTitle.context: Dialog heading when switching a multi-ministry claim to one ministry.

Review.modeSwitchBody.source: Every row will be set to {value} — the ministry most rows already use.
Review.modeSwitchBody.context: Dialog body; <strong>{value} is the adopted ministry. The ICU '=1' branch is DELIBERATELY EMPTY — the clause about N different ministries only appears when more than one is picked.

Review.modeSwitchBodyNoMinistry.source: Rows keep no ministry until you pick one at the top.
Review.modeSwitchBodyNoMinistry.context: Variant of that body when no row has a ministry yet.

Review.modeSwitchUnverify.source: {count, plural, one {# verified row} other {# verified rows}} will need re-verifying.
Review.modeSwitchUnverify.context: Amber warning inside that dialog: verified rows lose their check (ICU).

Review.panelTitleSingle.context: Heading of the ministry panel in single-ministry mode.

Review.panelTitle.context: Heading of the same panel in multi mode.

Review.modeOne.context: Left segment of the mode toggle; keep short. Review.multiHint quotes this wording (test-enforced).

Review.modeMultiple.context: Right segment of the mode toggle. One word if possible.

Review.descPlaceholder.source: What’s this claim for? e.g. “snacks for the youth retreat”
Review.descPlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the claim-description input; keep the quoted example phrasing.

Review.suggestTitle.source: Let the AI suggest a ministry & event from your description — you still apply it
Review.suggestTitle.context: Tooltip of the Suggest button: the AI proposes, the human applies.

Review.thinking.context: Suggest button label while the AI call runs. Keep the ellipsis.

Review.suggest.context: The AI-suggestion button; keep the ✨. Keep short.

Review.suggestionApplied.context: Suggestion-banner prefix after applying; <strong>{value} is the ministry — event.

Review.suggestionSuggested.context: Suggestion-banner prefix before applying; <strong>{value} as above.

Review.applyAllRows.context: Banner button on multi-receipt claims: mirror the suggestion onto every row.

Review.apply.context: Same button on single-receipt claims. One word.

Review.dismiss.context: Banner link that discards the suggestion. One word.

Review.noConfidentMatch.source: No confident match — pick a ministry below.
Review.noConfidentMatch.context: Banner when the AI declines to pick from the chart of accounts.

Review.pickMinistry.context: Empty first <option> of every ministry select; keep the '— … —' dash style.

Review.otherOption.context: Last <option>, opening a free-text ministry input; keep the ellipsis.

Review.claimMinistryAria.context: aria-label of the CLAIM-level ministry select. MUST stay distinct from Review.ministryAria (row-level) — tests and screen readers rely on it.

Review.customClaimMinistryAria.context: aria-label of the claim-level free-text ministry input; keep distinct from Review.customMinistryAria.

Review.eventPlaceholder.context: Placeholder of the event inputs; keep the (optional) suffix. An event example: a retreat, VBS.

Review.claimEventAria.context: aria-label of the claim-level event input; keep distinct from Review.eventAria.

Review.appliedEveryRow.source: Applied to every row — you still confirm each amount below.
Review.appliedEveryRow.context: Fine print under the claim-level controls: values mirror to rows, amounts still need per-row confirmation.

Review.multiHint.source: Each row picks its own ministry below. Switch to “One ministry” to set them all at once.
Review.multiHint.context: Fine print in multi mode. Quotes the One-ministry toggle — its exact translation is provided as mustContain and must appear verbatim (test-enforced).

Review.rowDescPlaceholder.context: Placeholder of each row's description textarea.

Review.rowDescAria.context: aria-label of that textarea. One word.

Review.badgeTitle.source: Ministry applies claim-wide — set it at the top
Review.badgeTitle.context: Tooltip on a row's ministry badge in single mode: change it at the top.

Review.ministrySetAbove.context: Badge text when the row has no ministry yet; ↑ points at the panel above — keep it.

Review.ministryAria.context: aria-label of the ROW-level ministry select. MUST stay distinct from Review.claimMinistryAria. One word.

Review.customMinistryAria.context: aria-label of the row-level free-text ministry input.

Review.eventAria.context: aria-label of the row-level event input. One word.

Review.amountAria.context: aria-label of the row's dollar-amount input; a literal $ sits before the box. One word.

Review.refundBadge.context: Tiny red badge on negative rows. English is uppercase for loudness; keep it very short.

Review.splitRowTitle.context: Tooltip of the Split button: divide one receipt between ministries.

Review.splitButton.context: Row action; keep the ⑂. Glossary: 拆分. Keep short.

Review.mergeBlockedTitle.source: Restore the excluded row before merging
Review.mergeBlockedTitle.context: Tooltip of Merge when the row above is excluded.

Review.mergeTitle.source: Merge back into the row above (undo split)
Review.mergeTitle.context: Tooltip of Merge: fold this split half back into the row above.

Review.mergeButton.context: Row action; keep the ⤴. Keep short.

Review.restoreTitle.context: Tooltip of the exclude button when the row is already excluded.

Review.excludeTitle.context: Tooltip of the exclude button: drop a personal / non-reimbursable item.

Review.restoreButton.context: Row action un-excluding an item; keep the ↩.

Review.excludeButton.context: Row action excluding an item; keep the 🗑.

Review.nudgeVerify.context: Pulsing hint pointing (→) at the confirm button when it's ready to click.

Review.nudgeSetAbove.source: Set the ministry at the top, then verify →
Review.nudgeSetAbove.context: Same hint on single-ministry claims when no ministry is set yet; keep the →.

Review.nudgePick.context: Same hint on multi-ministry claims; keep the →.

Review.verifyTitleVerified.context: Tooltip of the confirm button once verified (clicking undoes).

Review.verifyTitle.source: Click to certify that description, ministry, and amount are correct.
Review.verifyTitle.context: Tooltip of the confirm button: this click is the human attestation.

Review.looksCorrect.context: THE verification button on every row — 'I checked this against the receipt'. Keep short; a ✓ checkbox renders before it.

Review.splitTotal.context: Line showing the row's total; {amount} is a pre-formatted $ value.

Review.firstPart.context: Input label for the first half's amount; keep the ($).

Review.secondPart.context: Computed remainder line; <strong>{amount} is pre-formatted (or — while invalid).

Review.splitFailed.context: Fallback error when the split fails.

Review.splitConfirm.context: Primary button of the split dialog. One word; pairs with Review.splitButton.

Review.underSignatureFrozen.source: Under signature — packet and rows are frozen.
Review.underSignatureFrozen.context: Note replacing the row toolbar once a claim is submitted for e-signature: the PDF packet and rows can no longer change.

Review.downloadSigned.context: Download button once a claim is under signature — downloads the archived signed packet instead of regenerating. Keep the ⬇.

Review.revertConfirmSigned.source: Revert this claim to draft? Every signature collected so far becomes void, and approval starts over.
Review.revertConfirmSigned.context: confirm() before reverting a claim that has e-signatures: warns that collected signatures become void and approval restarts.

Esign.submitForApproval.context: Primary button opening the submit-for-approval signing dialog. Keep the ✍️ prefix.

Esign.panelSubmitted.context: Panel heading while the claim waits for the approver.

Esign.panelRejected.context: Panel heading after the approver rejected the claim.

Esign.panelApproved.context: Panel heading after approval, before the treasurer pays.

Esign.panelPaid.context: Panel heading once paid (no check number recorded).

Esign.panelPaidWithCheck.context: Panel heading once paid; {checkNumber} is the check number the treasurer typed.

Esign.approverComment.context: Shows the approver's rejection comment; {comment} is their text. Keep the quotation marks around it.

Esign.certificateLink.context: Link to download the PDF approval certificate. Keep the 📜.

Esign.withdrawConfirm.source: Withdraw this submission so you can pick a different approver?
Esign.withdrawConfirm.context: confirm() before withdrawing a submission to pick a different approver.

Esign.withdrawButton.context: Button to withdraw the pending submission / change approver. Keep the ↪.

Esign.resubmit.context: Button to reopen the signing dialog after a rejection. Keep the ✍️.

Esign.reverify.context: Button that re-runs the signature-chain check. Plain wording (not "verify") keeps verifiability low-key. Keep the ⟳.

Esign.verifyingChain.context: Progress line while the browser re-checks all signatures. Plain wording (not "verifying the signature chain") keeps verifiability low-key. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.failClosed.source: Signing is disabled until everything checks out.
Esign.failClosed.context: Amber note under the banner when verification hasn't fully passed: the Approve/Reject buttons stay disabled.

Esign.openPacketButton.context: Button opening the full reimbursement PDF (form pages + appended receipts) the approver/treasurer reviews before signing. "reimbursement" not "verified" — the packet's the point, not its verifiability. Keep the 📄.

Esign.openApprovedPacketButton.source: 🖋️ Open approved packet — with approver's signature

Esign.submitDialogTitle.context: Title of the signing dialog. Must read the same as the ✍️ Submit for approval button (without the emoji).

Esign.notAttested.source: You can't sign yet — turn on signing on your <link>profile</link>, then get vouched.
Esign.notAttested.context: Shown in the signing dialog when the member isn't vouched yet. <link> wraps the word for the profile page.

Esign.submitIntro.source: You're asking for approval of this claim ({amount}). Place your signature on the form, pick who should approve it, and sign. If the claim changes later, your signature stops counting and approval starts over.
Esign.submitIntro.context: Explains the submit ceremony. {amount} is the claim total. Plain, non-technical wording.

Esign.openingForm.context: Placeholder while the PDF loads into the signing view. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.approverLabel.context: Label of the approver dropdown in the signing dialog.

Esign.approverPlaceholder.context: Empty first option of that dropdown. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.typedNameLabel.source: Type your full name to sign
Esign.typedNameLabel.context: Label of the type-your-full-name field — typing the name is part of the legal signature.

Esign.consentSummary.context: Collapsed <details> summary hiding the legal consent text.

Esign.consentEnglishNote.source: The legally binding consent text is the English version below.
Esign.consentEnglishNote.context: Small note above the consent document explaining that the binding text is the English original (it is hash-bound and cannot be translated).

Esign.intentAffirmation.source: I intend this to be my signature.
Esign.intentAffirmation.context: Checkbox label the signer must tick — a legal affirmation of intent to sign. Keep it a serious, first-person statement.

Esign.signAndSubmit.context: Confirm button of the submit ceremony (signs and sends to the approver).

Esign.signing.context: Busy label while a signature is being created. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.submissionFailed.context: Fallback error if the submit ceremony fails without a specific message.

Esign.ceremonyFailed.context: Fallback error if an approve/reject/paid ceremony fails without a specific message.

Esign.loadFailed.context: Fallback error when a list (approvals, finance, vouch) fails to load.

Esign.connectBody.source: Sign in with your church Google account to sign securely. You only need to do this once on this device.

Esign.connectBlocked.source: Your browser blocked the sign-in window. Tap “Connect signing” again to allow it.

Esign.itemsCount.context: Fallback list-row description when a claim has no description: its line-item count.

Esign.bannerOk.source: ✓ Everything checks out — this is the genuine paperwork, unchanged.
Esign.bannerOk.context: Reassurance line at the top of the Audit details disclosure (not shown on the main path — a clean chain stays quiet). Written for non-technical members. Keep the leading ✓.

Esign.bannerBad.source: ✗ Something doesn't check out. Don't sign — ask the person who sent this (details below for whoever helps you).
Esign.bannerBad.context: Red alert — the ONLY chain banner shown on the main path, and only when a check fails: tells the member not to sign and to ask for help. Keep the leading ✗.

Esign.auditDetails.context: Collapsed <details> summary hiding cryptographic detail (fingerprints, hashes). Used on several e-sign screens.

Esign.pillRootPinned.context: Green pill inside audit details: the church root key matched the pinned value. {by} says what pinned it (config/device).

Esign.pillRootFailed.context: Red pill: the root key did NOT match. Keep it alarming (capitals in English).

Esign.pillChain.context: Pill label for the signature-chain check.

Esign.pillPacket.context: Pill label for the packet-bytes hash check.

Esign.anomaliesHeader.source: {count, plural, one {# invalid event on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):} other {# invalid events on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):}}
Esign.anomaliesHeader.context: Audit list header when invalid ledger events exist. {count} plural. 'Kept visible' = the app never hides tampering.

Esign.independentCheck.source: Independent check: download the certificate and run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs with the church's published root fingerprint.
Esign.independentCheck.context: Audit note telling a technical auditor how to verify offline. Keep the script name inside <code> untranslated.

Esign.signerFingerprints.context: Audit list header for each signer's key fingerprint.

Esign.unknownMember.context: Placeholder when a signer's key is not on the roster (lowercase, inline).

Esign.blockSubmitted.context: Signature-block title: the requester's submit signature. {seq} is the submission attempt number.

Esign.blockApproved.context: Signature-block title when the decision was approval.

Esign.blockRejected.context: Signature-block title when the decision was rejection.

Esign.blockPaid.context: Signature-block title for the treasurer's payment signature.

Esign.checkNumber.context: Line under the payment signature; {number} is the check number.

Esign.unknownSigner.context: Shown instead of a name when the signer isn't identifiable.

Esign.tapToSign.context: Pulsing yellow tab ON the document's signature line — pressing and HOLDING it stamps the signature (the legally required act). Keep the ✍️ and keep it short.

Esign.holdingToSign.context: Label shown on the sign tab while the signer is mid-hold and a progress ring is filling. Keep it short.

Esign.signedDragHint.source: Signed. Drag your signature if you want it somewhere else.
Esign.signedDragHint.context: Hint after the signature is stamped: it can be dragged to reposition.

Esign.holdTabHint.source: Press and hold the yellow tab to sign on the line.
Esign.holdTabHint.context: Hint before stamping: tells the signer to press and hold the yellow tab.

Esign.dragFineTune.context: Small hint below the document while the stamp is placed.

Esign.removeSignature.context: Small link that removes the stamped signature (returns to the hold-to-sign state).

Esign.appearsAfterTap.source: Your signature appears after you hold the yellow tab.
Esign.appearsAfterTap.context: Small reassurance below the document before the signer has held the tab.

Esign.zoomIn.context: aria-label/tooltip for the zoom-in (+) button on the document preview.

Esign.zoomOut.context: aria-label/tooltip for the zoom-out (−) button on the document preview.

Esign.zoomReset.context: aria-label/tooltip for the button (shows the current zoom %) that resets the document preview to 100%.

Esign.couldNotOpenDoc.source: Couldn't open the document to sign. {message}
Esign.couldNotOpenDoc.context: Error box when the PDF can't be rendered; {message} is the technical reason (may stay English).

Esign.openingDoc.context: Placeholder while the PDF renders. Keep the ellipsis.

Esign.docAlt.context: alt text of the rendered document image.

Esign.signatureAlt.context: alt text of the member's hand-drawn signature image.

Esign.signAbove.source: Sign above with your finger or mouse
Esign.signAbove.context: Caption under the empty drawing canvas (draw with finger or mouse).

Esign.clearPad.context: Link that wipes the drawing canvas.

Approvals.title.context: Page title of the approver's inbox; also the nav link.

Approvals.subtitle.source: Open a claim to check it and sign.
Approvals.subtitle.context: One-line instruction under the inbox title.

Approvals.empty.context: Empty-state line when nothing awaits the approver (friendly, next to a 🕊️).

Approvals.decided.context: Section header (uppercase style) listing already-decided claims.

Approvals.submittedOn.context: List-row suffix; {date} is a formatted date. Lowercase inline fragment after a ·.

Approvals.commentLabel.context: Label of the comment field; a comment is mandatory only for rejection.

Approvals.rejectNeedsComment.source: Add a comment explaining the rejection
Approvals.rejectNeedsComment.context: Tooltip on the disabled Reject button until a comment is typed.

Approvals.approveAndSign.context: Approve button — approving IS signing. Keep the ✓.

Finance.title.context: Page title of the treasurer's queue; also the nav link.

Finance.subtitle.context: One-line description under the finance title.

Finance.empty.context: Empty-state line when no approved claims await payment (next to a 🧮).

Finance.certificate.context: Small lowercase link to the approval certificate in the paid list.

Finance.checkNumberLabel.context: Label of the optional check-number field in the mark-paid ceremony.

Finance.signAndMarkPaid.context: Confirm button of the payment ceremony. Keep the 💸.

Identity.title.context: Heading of the profile-page card for electronic signing.

Identity.chipReady.context: Status chip: fully vouched, can sign.

Identity.chipPending.context: Status chip: enrolled, still needs vouches. Encouraging tone.

Identity.chipRevoked.context: Status chip: the signing key was revoked.

Identity.chipNone.context: Status chip: never set up signing.

Identity.loadFailed.context: Error when the signing status can't be loaded.

Identity.switchFailed.context: Error when flipping the master switch fails.

Identity.switchOn.context: Bold line of the admin's master-switch row when e-signing is on. ON is deliberately loud.

Identity.switchOff.context: Same row when off. OFF is deliberately loud.

Identity.switchOnBody.source: Members can enroll, submit, approve, and pay electronically.
Identity.switchOnBody.context: Small print under the ON state: what members can now do.

Identity.switchOffBody.source: Off for everyone (the default). Existing signed records stay verifiable.
Identity.switchOffBody.context: Small print under the OFF state: off is the default; old signed records stay verifiable.

Identity.bootstrapIntro.source: You're the person who sets up electronic signing for this church. It starts switched off, so nothing changes for members until you turn it on.
Identity.bootstrapIntro.context: Shown only to the designated first admin before setup. <b> wraps 'off'. Explains setup creates the registry with them as anchor.

Identity.bootstrapButton.context: Button that performs the one-time setup.

Identity.bootstrapBusy.context: Busy label during setup. Keep the ellipsis.

Identity.notSetUp.source: Electronic signing isn't set up for this church yet — ask your administrator.
Identity.notSetUp.context: Shown to members while the church hasn't set up e-signing.

Identity.enrollIntro.source: Sign and approve reimbursements from your phone — no printing. You'll agree to sign electronically, draw your signature, and then two members confirm it's really you (in person, one quick scan).
Identity.enrollIntro.context: Pitch shown before a member enrolls: agree → draw signature → two members confirm in person. Friendly, non-technical.

Identity.checkingDevice.context: Placeholder while the app checks whether this browser holds the member's keys. Keep the ellipsis.

Identity.yourSignature.context: Small label above the saved hand-drawn signature image.

Identity.redraw.context: Small link to redraw the saved signature.

Identity.addSignature.context: Button when no drawn signature exists yet. Keep the ✍️.

Identity.pendingVouch.source: One last step: show this to two members (or one approver) in person. They open Vouch for a member, scan it, and confirm it's really you.
Identity.pendingVouch.context: Amber instructions above the QR while awaiting vouches. <b> wraps 'in person'. Two members or one approver must scan.

Identity.vouchForMember.context: Link/button to the vouching page (shown to attested members). Keep the 🤝.

Identity.roleLabel.context: Audit-details line label; the member's church role follows it.

Identity.yourFingerprint.context: Audit-details line label; the member's key fingerprint follows.

Identity.fullFingerprint.context: Parenthetical giving the untruncated fingerprint; {fp} is 64 hex chars.

Identity.rootFingerprint.context: Audit-details line label for the church's root key fingerprint.

Identity.compareNote.context: Dash-note after the root fingerprint: compare it against the value the church published.

Identity.openLink.context: Compact label on the candidate's vouching QR — a link to the /vouch URL for a voucher whose phone camera opens it directly. Replaces the previously displayed full URL.

Identity.manualFallbackTitle.context: Summary of a disclosure under the candidate's vouching QR, revealing the key fingerprint to read out when the camera can't scan.

Identity.manualFallbackBody.source: Have your voucher pick you from the list on their Vouch screen and type this key fingerprint by hand:
Identity.manualFallbackBody.context: Body of the camera-less fallback: the voucher picks the candidate from the pending list and types the full key fingerprint (never the short spoken code). Mirrors Vouch.scanIntro.

Identity.copyCode.context: Button that copies the key fingerprint to the clipboard.

Identity.copiedCode.context: Transient confirmation shown on the copy button after the fingerprint is copied.

Identity.qrAria.context: aria-label of the vouching QR code image.

Identity.consentTitle.source: Sign electronically instead of on paper
Identity.consentTitle.context: Title of the consent step of the enrollment wizard.

Identity.consentIntro.source: Please read this once — it says your electronic signature counts like an ink one, and that you can always go back to paper.
Identity.consentIntro.context: Friendly summary above the legal text: e-signature counts like ink; paper remains possible.

Identity.consentNext.context: Button advancing from consent to the drawing step.

Identity.drawIntro.source: This is the signature that appears on the reimbursement forms — sign the way you would on paper, with your finger or the mouse.
Identity.drawIntro.context: Instruction above the canvas: this drawing appears on the forms; sign like on paper.

Identity.finishSetup.context: Final wizard button (creates the signing key).

Identity.finishing.context: Busy label for that button. Keep the ellipsis.

Identity.redrawBody.source: Your new signature is used on everything you sign from now on. Nothing you've already signed changes.
Identity.redrawBody.context: Note in that dialog: the new drawing affects future paperwork only.

Identity.saveSignature.context: Save button of the redraw dialog.

Identity.couldNotSave.context: Fallback error for the redraw dialog.

Identity.scopeTitle.context: Heading of the admin's rollout-scope row (allowlist vs everyone), under the master switch.

Identity.scopeAllowlist.context: Scope button: restrict e-signing to the allowlist below. Keep short — segmented button.

Identity.scopeEveryone.context: Scope button: open e-signing to all signed-in users. Keep short — segmented button.

Identity.allowlistTitle.source: Members who can use electronic signing
Identity.allowlistTitle.context: Heading of the admin's allowlist panel.

Identity.allowlistNote.source: People you allow can see the signing features and enroll, but they still need to be vouched for before they can sign. Removing someone hides signing from them; anything they already signed stays valid.
Identity.allowlistNote.context: Explainer under the allowlist heading. The second sentence is the important retention promise.

Identity.allowlistAllow.context: Button: add this member to the e-sign allowlist. Keep short.

Identity.allowlistRemove.context: Button: take this member off the e-sign allowlist. Keep short.

Identity.allowlistAlwaysOn.context: Chip on the admin's own row in the allowlist: admins cannot be excluded.

Identity.allowlistLoadFailed.source: Could not load the member list
Identity.allowlistLoadFailed.context: Client-side fallback error for the allowlist panel.

Identity.allowlistUpdateFailed.context: Client-side fallback error when an allow/remove toggle fails.

Devices.newDeviceTitle.source: This looks like a new device.
Devices.newDeviceTitle.context: Amber card title when a signed-in member opens the app on a browser that doesn't hold their keys.

Devices.newDeviceBody.source: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this device yet. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed.
Devices.newDeviceBody.context: Body of that card (their fleet still exists): bring the identity over; no re-vouching needed.

Devices.newDeviceBodyGone.source: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this account anymore. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed unless you start over.
Devices.newDeviceBodyGone.context: Variant when the whole fleet was wiped (post start-over): only starting over remains, unless they start over.

Devices.approveFromOther.source: 📱 Approve from a device you already sign with
Devices.approveFromOther.context: Primary recovery path button: approve this browser from a device that already signs. Keep the 📱.

Devices.typePhraseOption.context: Secondary path button: type the 24-word recovery phrase. Keep the 🔑.

Devices.keysRemovedEverywhere.source: Your signing keys were removed everywhere, so the only way forward is to start over below.
Devices.keysRemovedEverywhere.context: Shown instead of the two buttons when no device holds keys anymore.

Devices.noneWork.context: Collapsed <details> summary of the last-resort help.

Devices.noneWorkBody.source: If your old devices are gone and you never printed a recovery sheet, your identity can't be recovered — that's what keeps it yours. Starting over creates a brand-new signing key: the same people vouch for you again, in person, and the moment they do, your old key stops counting on its own. Worried someone might use the old key before then? Tell your administrator and they can retire it right away.
Devices.noneWorkBody.context: Last-resort explanation: unrecoverable by design; starting over re-vouches and auto-retires the old key; tell the admin only if someone might USE the old key.

Devices.startOver.context: Deliberately buried destructive button: discard the identity and enroll fresh.

Devices.startOverConfirm.source:
Start over from nothing?

This signs ALL your devices out and throws away your current signing key. You'll set up again from scratch and be vouched for again in person — which automatically retires the old key. Anything you already signed stays valid.
Devices.startOverConfirm.context: confirm() for start-over. Keep the blank line (\n\n). Serious but calm: old signatures stay valid.

Devices.couldNotStart.context: Error when the device-approval request can't be created.

Devices.waitingIntro.source: Now pick up the device you already sign with. A message appears there — type this code into it:
Devices.waitingIntro.context: Instruction above the big 6-digit code: pick up the other device and type this code there.

Devices.waitingNote.source: Waiting for the other device… this page continues by itself once it's approved.
Devices.waitingNote.context: Small print while waiting; <em> wraps 'this'. Explains the code binds the approval to this browser.

Devices.phrasePlaceholder.context: Textarea placeholder — an example of the phrase format. Recovery phrases are always English BIP39 words: keep the example words in English.

Devices.phraseWrong.source: That phrase didn't work — check the words and their order
Devices.phraseWrong.context: Error when the typed phrase doesn't unlock: check words and their order.

Devices.unlock.context: Submit button of the phrase form.

Devices.deviceWants.context: Banner headline when a new device asks to be approved; {name} is the device's self-reported name, already in quotes.

Devices.approveHint.source: Only approve if it's yours and in your hands. Type the 6-digit code from its screen — that's what proves it's really that device.
Devices.approveHint.context: Banner small print: only approve your own device in your hands; type the code from ITS screen (<em> wraps 'its').

Devices.approve.context: Approve button of the device banner.

Devices.rejectDevice.context: Reject button of the device banner.

Devices.approvalFailed.context: Error when approving the device fails (usually a wrong code).

Devices.devicesTitle.source: Devices that can sign as you
Devices.devicesTitle.context: Small header of the authorized-devices list on the profile card.

Devices.thisDevice.context: Green chip marking the browser you're reading this on.

Devices.addedOn.context: Small suffix per device; {date} is a formatted date. Lowercase fragment.

Devices.removeDevice.context: Small lowercase button removing a device from the list.

Devices.removeConfirm.source:
Sign “{name}” out of electronic signing?

It loses access right away. If the device might be in someone else's hands, tell your administrator in person too, so they can revoke your signing key.
Devices.removeConfirm.context: confirm() for removing a device; {name} is the device name. Keep the blank line (\n\n).

Devices.lostHint.source: Lost one? Remove it here, then tell your administrator in person if someone else might have it — they can revoke the signing key itself.
Devices.lostHint.context: Small print under the device list: remove lost devices; tell the admin in person if someone else might have it.

Devices.recoveryRoot.source: Protect the church's signing anchor. If this device is lost with no recovery sheet, electronic signing has to be rebuilt from scratch for everyone.
Devices.recoveryRoot.context: Sticky amber nudge for the ADMIN: the root key anchors everyone — print the recovery sheet.

Devices.recoveryNudge.source: One more safety net: print a recovery sheet and you can sign from a new device even if this one is lost.
Devices.recoveryNudge.context: Dismissible amber nudge for members: print a recovery sheet as a safety net.

Devices.preparing.context: Placeholder while the phrase is generated. Keep the ellipsis.

Devices.sheetBody.source: This one-page sheet holds the 24 words that can bring your signing identity to a new device. Print it and keep it with your important papers at home. Anyone holding the sheet can sign as you — and nobody, including the church, can recreate it if it's lost.
Devices.sheetBody.context: Explains the sheet: 24 words, keep at home with important papers; anyone holding it can sign as you; nobody can recreate it.

Devices.downloadSheet.context: Download button for the printable PDF. Keep the 🖨️.

Devices.downloadedPrint.context: Same button after downloading: now print it. Keep the ✓.

Devices.noPrinter.source: No printer? Copy the words by hand

Devices.savedCheckbox.source: The sheet is printed (or written out) and stored somewhere safe.
Devices.savedCheckbox.context: Checkbox the member must tick before closing: the sheet is printed/written and stored safely.

Devices.done.context: Final button of the recovery dialog.

Devices.couldNotBuildSheet.context: Error when building the PDF fails.

Vouch.title.context: Page title of the in-person vouching screen. Must read the same as the 🤝 Vouch for a member link (without the emoji).

Vouch.onlyAttested.source: Only attested members can vouch. Enable signing on your <link>profile</link> and get vouched first.
Vouch.onlyAttested.context: Shown when the visitor can't vouch yet; <link> wraps the word for the profile page.

Vouch.doneTitle.context: Success line after signing a vouch; {name} is the candidate.

Vouch.doneBody.source: They need two member vouches (or one from an approver) to become attested.
Vouch.doneBody.context: Reminder of the attestation rule: two member vouches, or one from an approver.

Vouch.beforeTitle.source: Before you vouch, confirm in person:
Vouch.beforeTitle.context: Bold header of the amber in-person checklist.

Vouch.bullet1.source: The person is physically with you right now.
Vouch.bullet1.context: Checklist: the person is physically present.

Vouch.bullet2.source: This name and email really belong to them.
Vouch.bullet2.context: Checklist: the name and email really belong to them.

Vouch.bullet3.source: You scanned the QR from THEIR screen (not a forwarded link).
Vouch.bullet3.context: Checklist: the QR was scanned from THEIR screen, not a forwarded link.

Vouch.rekeyTitle.context: Warning when the vouch would replace an existing key; {name} is the candidate.

Vouch.rekeyBody.source: Usually that means a lost device. The moment they're vouched back in, the old key stops counting — everything they signed before stays valid. Be extra sure it's really them.
Vouch.rekeyBody.context: Explains re-keying: usually a lost device; old key stops counting once re-vouched; old signatures stay valid; be extra sure.

Vouch.manualLabel.source: Type their full key fingerprint (from their screen — the spoken 6-digit code is never enough):
Vouch.manualLabel.context: Label of the manual fingerprint field (no-QR fallback). The spoken 6-digit code is never sufficient.

Vouch.manualPlaceholder.context: Placeholder describing the expected input: 64 hex characters, at least 32 accepted.

Vouch.fpMismatch.context: Inline error while the typed fingerprint doesn't match.

Vouch.confirmLabel.source: I am with {name} in person and confirm this identity is theirs.
Vouch.confirmLabel.context: The vouch checkbox — a first-person statement of in-person identity confirmation. <b> wraps {name}.

Vouch.scanIntro.source: Scan the candidate's QR right here with your camera. Can't use the camera? Pick them from the list and type their full key fingerprint (read from their screen; the short spoken code is not enough).
Vouch.scanIntro.context: Intro on the vouch page without a QR parameter: scan with the phone camera, or pick a candidate and type the full fingerprint (<b> wraps it).

Vouch.orPick.source: Or pick them and type their fingerprint

Vouch.nobodyPending.context: Empty state of the awaiting-vouches list.

Vouch.roleLabel.context: Label before the role select in the attested-members list.

Vouch.roleDialogTitle.context: Confirm-dialog title for changing a member's role; {name} is the member.

Vouch.roleDialogFromTo.source: This changes their role from {from} to {to}.
Vouch.roleDialogFromTo.context: Confirm-dialog line; {from}/{to} are translated role names (Member/Approver/Treasurer). Keep the <b> tags.

Vouch.roleExplainMember.source: Members can prepare and sign their own reimbursements, but can't approve or pay others'.
Vouch.roleExplainMember.context: Confirm-dialog blurb: what a plain member can do.

Vouch.roleExplainApprover.source: Approvers can approve reimbursement claims for payment.
Vouch.roleExplainApprover.context: Confirm-dialog blurb: what an approver can do.

Vouch.roleExplainTreasurer.source: Treasurers can approve claims and also mark them paid.
Vouch.roleExplainTreasurer.context: Confirm-dialog blurb: what a treasurer can do (approver powers plus marking paid).

Vouch.roleDialogConfirm.context: Confirm button on the role-change dialog.

Vouch.revokeKey.context: Signing-key action button, and the confirm button on its dialog.

Vouch.keyDialogTitle.context: Confirm-dialog title for revoking a member's signing key; {name} is the member.

Vouch.keyDialogBody.source: Do this only when a device that could sign as them was lost or stolen. Everything they've already signed stays valid, but the key stops working everywhere — they'll set up signing again and get vouched back in person.
Vouch.keyDialogBody.context: Confirm-dialog body explaining what revoking the signing key does. Old signatures stay valid.

Vouch.vouchFailed.context: Fallback error when signing the vouch fails.

Vouch.malformedLink.source: This vouch link is malformed — rescan the QR
Vouch.malformedLink.context: Error when the scanned vouch URL is corrupt: rescan the QR.

VouchScan.aim.source: Point at the candidate's QR code

VouchScan.notVouch.source: That's not a vouch code — line up the candidate's QR code.

VouchScan.insecure.source: This device can't open the camera here. Use the list below instead.

VouchScan.denied.source: Camera access was blocked. Allow it in your browser settings, or use the list below.

VouchScan.startFailed.source: Couldn't start the camera. Use the list below instead.

Verify.verifying.context: Loading line while signatures are re-checked in the visitor's browser. Keep the ellipsis.

Verify.failedTitle.context: Red card title when verification can't complete.

Verify.linkInvalid.source: This verification link is not valid ({part})
Verify.linkInvalid.context: Error for a bad/expired verification link; {part} is a technical endpoint name (leave as-is).

Verify.verifiedTitle.context: Green card title when every check passed.

Verify.warningsTitle.context: Amber card title when checks finished but something is off.

Verify.claimByLine.context: Summary line; {name} is the requester (<b>-wrapped). Ends with a separator before 'checked just now'.

Verify.checkedNow.source: checked just now, in this browser.
Verify.checkedNow.context: End of the summary sentence: the verdict was computed just now, in this browser (not by the server).

Verify.rootPinnedCfg.context: Audit check label: the root key matched the deployment's pinned fingerprint.

Verify.rootRelayed.source: root relayed by this server (verify the fingerprint in person)
Verify.rootRelayed.context: Audit check label when no pin is configured: the server relayed the root — verify the fingerprint in person.

Verify.packetMatch.source: packet bytes match the signed hash ({hash}…)
Verify.packetMatch.context: Audit check label: the PDF bytes hash to the signed value; {hash} is a hash prefix.

Verify.packetMismatch.source: packet bytes do NOT match the signed hash
Verify.packetMismatch.context: Audit check label when the bytes do NOT match. Alarming.

Verify.noAnomalies.context: Audit check label: the ledger contains no invalid events.

Verify.anomalyCount.source: {count, plural, one {# ledger anomaly} other {# ledger anomalies}}
Verify.anomalyCount.context: Audit check label with {count} plural invalid events.

Verify.rosterOddities.source: {count, plural, one {# roster oddity} other {# roster oddities}}
Verify.rosterOddities.context: Audit check label with {count} plural roster irregularities.

Verify.invalidEvents.context: Header of the invalid-events list (kept visible on purpose).

Verify.independentCheckCert.source: Independent check: run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs against the certificate's embedded bundle with the church's published root fingerprint.
Verify.independentCheckCert.context: Note for technical auditors; keep the script name in <code> untranslated.

Verify.threadTitle.context: Card title per submission attempt; {seq} is the attempt number.

Verify.submitted.context: Signature line title: the requester's submission signature.

Verify.approved.context: Signature line title: the approval signature.

Verify.rejected.context: Signature line title: the rejection signature.

Verify.paid.context: Signature line title: the payment signature.

Verify.keySummary.context: Tiny <details> summary revealing the signer's key fingerprint (lowercase).

Verify.unknown.context: Fallback signer name when the key maps to nobody on the roster.

Verify.state.open.context: Thread state chip: submitted, awaiting decision.

Verify.state.withdrawn.context: Thread state chip: the requester withdrew this submission.

Verify.state.superseded.context: Thread state chip: replaced by a newer submission.

Errors.notSignedIn.context: 401 body when the session is missing/expired.

Errors.idTokenRequired.context: 'idToken' is an API field name — keep it verbatim.

Errors.verifiedEmailRequired.source: A verified email address is required

Errors.emailRequired.context: Dev-login validation; lowercase 'email' mirrors the API field name.

Errors.notFound.context: Deliberately vague 404 — used where existence must not leak (capability URLs, cross-tenant). Keep it generic.

Errors.receiptOnClaim.source: Receipt is part of a claim and cannot be deleted

Errors.unsupportedFileType.context: {type} is a raw MIME string like image/heic — keep the placeholder as-is.

Errors.previewPdfOnly.source: Preview is only available for PDF receipts

Errors.imageEditImagesOnly.source: Only image receipts can be rotated or cropped

Errors.receiptFrozenByClaim.source: This receipt is on a generated claim — revert that claim before editing the image
Errors.receiptFrozenByClaim.context: 409 for image edits while a generated claim holds the receipt.

Errors.nothingToRestore.source: This receipt has no earlier version to restore

Errors.cropTooSmall.source: Crop region is too small to stay legible
Errors.cropTooSmall.context: Rejects a crop too small to stay legible.

Errors.claimFrozen.source: Claim already generated; line items are frozen
Errors.claimFrozen.context: 409 for row edits on a generated claim; 'frozen' = locked until reverted to draft.

Errors.ministryRequiredToVerify.source: Choose a ministry before verifying this row

Errors.splitAmountsInvalid.source: Split amounts must be non-zero and sum to the original amount

Errors.nothingToMergeInto.source: No row above from the same receipt to merge into

Errors.restoreBeforeMerge.source: Restore the excluded row before merging

Errors.claimSettingsFrozen.source: Claim already generated; review settings are frozen

Errors.onlyDraftDeletable.source: Only draft claims can be deleted

Errors.receiptsNotFound.source: One or more receipts were not found

Errors.claimReceiptsFrozen.source: Claim already generated; receipts are frozen

Errors.receiptsAlreadyOnClaim.source: One or more receipts are already on this claim

Errors.claimEmpty.source: Claim has no line items to reimburse

Errors.rowsUnverified.source: {count} row(s) still need verification before the PDF can be generated
Errors.rowsUnverified.context: PDF gate message; {count} rows lack verification. English 'row(s)' is a pseudo-plural — Chinese needs none.

Errors.rowsMissingMinistry.source: {count} row(s) still need a ministry before the PDF can be generated
Errors.rowsMissingMinistry.context: PDF gate message; {count} rows lack a ministry.

Errors.receiptNotOnClaim.source: Receipt not found in this claim

Errors.rowAlreadyEdited.source: This receipt's row has already been edited — adjust it directly

Errors.lastReceiptOnClaim.source: This is the only receipt in the claim — discard the claim instead

Errors.onlyGeneratedRevertible.source: Only generated or under-signature claims can be reverted to draft
Errors.onlyGeneratedRevertible.context: 409 error. “Generated” and “under signature” are claim statuses — match the Common.status wording. Under-signature means submitted/rejected/approved (not yet paid).

Errors.descriptionRequired.source: Describe the claim in a sentence first

Errors.aiRateLimited.source: The AI provider is rate-limited right now — try again in a minute or pick a ministry manually

Errors.aiNoSuggestion.source: The AI couldn't produce a suggestion — pick a ministry manually

Errors.aiQuotaExhausted.source: The AI provider is busy right now — please wait a minute and try again. Affected: {names}
Errors.aiQuotaExhausted.context: 429 after retries; {rpm} requests/minute target, {names} is a list of receipt file names.

Errors.quotaWait.source: Rate limit reached — waiting {seconds}s before retrying ({attempt}/{maxRetries})…
Errors.quotaWait.context: Live status during a rate-limit wait; {seconds} countdown, ({attempt}/{maxRetries}) retry counter — keep the shape.

Errors.pdfFrozen.source: The packet is frozen under signature — download it from the claim page, or revert to draft to edit
Errors.pdfFrozen.context: 409 when regenerating a PDF that is under signature: download the archived packet or revert to draft.

Errors.esign.pickApprover.source: Pick an approver and type your name
Errors.esign.pickApprover.context: 400 in the submit ceremony: approver and typed name are required.

Errors.esign.placeSignature.source: Place your signature on the form first
Errors.esign.placeSignature.context: 400 in the submit ceremony: the drawn signature must be placed on the form first.

Errors.esign.typeNameApproval.source: Type your name to sign an approval
Errors.esign.typeNameApproval.context: 400: the approver's typed-name field is empty.

Errors.esign.selfApprove.source: You cannot approve your own claim
Errors.esign.selfApprove.context: 409: a requester picked themselves as approver.

Errors.esign.notApprover.source: That member is not an attested approver
Errors.esign.notApprover.context: 409: the chosen member isn't an attested approver.

Errors.esign.packetChanged.source: The packet changed since preflight — restart the ceremony
Errors.esign.packetChanged.context: 409: the PDF changed between preflight and signing — restart the ceremony.

Errors.esign.generateFirst.source: Generate the packet PDF before submitting
Errors.esign.generateFirst.context: 409: the claim has no generated PDF yet.

Errors.esign.mirrorBehind.source: Ledger mirror is behind — reconcile this claim first
Errors.esign.mirrorBehind.context: 409: the server's mirror lags the signature ledger; reconcile first ('reconcile' is a background sync the UI performs).

Errors.esign.noPacket.source: No packet for this claim yet
Errors.esign.noPacket.context: 404: no PDF packet exists for the claim yet.

Errors.esign.certNotReady.source: Certificates exist once a claim is approved
Errors.esign.certNotReady.context: 409: certificates only exist once a claim is approved.

Errors.esign.notEnrolled.context: 404: the member has no signing identity yet.

Errors.esign.notSetUp.source: E-sign is not set up yet
Errors.esign.notSetUp.context: 404: the church hasn't set up e-signing.

Errors.esign.switchedOff.context: 409 from every e-sign route while the admin master switch is off.

Errors.esign.notAllowed.source: Electronic signing is not enabled for your account
Errors.esign.notAllowed.context: 409 from e-sign routes when the rollout scope is allowlist and this member is not on it.

AddReceipts.title.context: Dialog heading: add receipts to an existing draft claim.

AddReceipts.intro.source: Pick from your existing receipts or upload new ones — each is read with AI and gets its own row to verify.
AddReceipts.intro.context: Dialog subtitle: pick from receipts already uploaded, or upload new files.

AddReceipts.upload.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.upload — translate that key.

AddReceipts.uploading.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.uploading — translate that key.

AddReceipts.emptyTitle.context: Empty state when every uploaded receipt is already on this claim.

AddReceipts.emptyBody.source: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to add it.

AddReceipts.manualInstead.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.manualInstead — translate that key.

AddReceipts.addingManual.context: Status while adding rows without AI.

AddReceipts.readingInitial.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.readingInitial — translate that key.

AddReceipts.readingCount.source: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
AddReceipts.readingCount.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.readingCount — translate that key.

AddReceipts.readProgress.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.readProgress — translate that key.

AddReceipts.loadFailed.context: Error when the Shoebox listing fails to load.

AddReceipts.uploadFailed.context: Auto-copied from Shoebox.uploadFailed — translate that key.

AddReceipts.addFailed.context: Error when adding the receipts to the claim fails.

AddReceipts.endedUnexpectedly.context: Error when the progress stream ends without a result.

AddReceipts.confirmReading.context: Primary button while extraction runs. Keep the ellipsis.

AddReceipts.confirmCount.source: ✨ Add {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}
AddReceipts.confirmCount.context: Primary button with a selection (ICU count); keep the ✨.

AddReceipts.confirm.context: Primary button with no selection yet; keep the ✨.
